questions.scenario: A second run reports no changes after the first run created the expected bucket and role. What does that suggest?

options.text: The desired state and reality now match.
The files are no longer useful.

questions.explanation: A quiet second run is useful evidence that the tool can avoid unnecessary changes.

questions.scenario: A task runs `mkdir /var/orders/exports` every time and fails if the directory already exists. What is the issue?

options.text: The task is not safely idempotent.
Directories cannot be managed as infrastructure.
All shell commands are forbidden in every situation.

questions.explanation: An idempotent task should check or declare the desired directory state so reruns are safe.

questions.scenario: Why does idempotency matter in real infrastructure work?

options.text: Provider APIs or networks may fail partway through.
Humans may retry after fixing a mistake.
Reruns should always create extra copies.

questions.explanation: Retries are normal, so automation should converge safely instead of duplicating or failing unnecessarily.
